reasoning: I will provide a concise summary of the key financial results, expansion efforts, and analyst commentary from the article.
output: Inditex reported robust results for the first half to 31 July: net earnings of €1.26bn, up 8%, and sales of €10.5bn, up 11% from €9.4bn. The group expanded its online presence to around 40 countries, with 83 new stores opened in 38 countries across its eight brands in 90 markets. It also highlighted technology investments, including online expansions to 11 new countries in the period and the launch of mobile payments in Spain, with plans for a group-wide InWallet app to integrate online and offline shopping. Analysts, such as Tom Gadsby of Liberum, say online growth could reduce the need for new stores over time while noting opportunities to expand online into new non-European territories. The company benefited from steady growth in Spain and stated it would open 6–8% of new store space over the year, contrasting with rivals H&M and Next, which blamed unseasonal weather for weaker results.
